Title: Anwar's Russia, Turkmenistan visits helped lower diesel prices, says Fahmi
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The reduction in subsidised diesel prices announced last week is a direct dividend of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's working visits to Russia and Turkmenistan, said unity government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
Fahmi said the Kazan leg of the trip secured a 20-year energy security agreement covering crude oil, gas and diesel, while the Ashgabat visit saw Petronas awarded two gas wells to manage and develop, cementing its place among the world's largest gas operators.
"This is an extraordinary success, and the impact is a source of pride not only for Petronas, which has had a presence in Turkmenistan for more than 30 years, but also for Malaysians, who will benefit from the returns through dividends channelled into national development," he told reporters at a press conference at the communications ministry today.
He said these energy security gains were closely tied to Anwar's June 21 announcement of a
cut in the price
of subsidised diesel.
"Arising from these two matters, energy security, and the opportunity to manage and develop among the largest gas wells in the world, the Prime Minister also announced a reduction in the price of diesel. So we see that these three matters, God willing, will have a good effect on the people's economy," he said.
Separately, asked whether the cabinet had discussed how the government planned to finance an anticipated increase in subsidies and cash aid in 2026 while remaining committed to narrowing the fiscal deficit, he said the matter would be addressed at the upcoming National Economic Action Council (NEAC) meeting, likely in mid-July.
Anwar had earlier indicated that subsidies and cash assistance
would increase
in 2026 even as the government remains committed to fiscal consolidation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hong Leong issues RM640mil Tier 2 subordinated notes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Bank Bhd (HLB) has issued subordinated notes worth RM640 million as part of its multi-currency Tier 2 subordinated notes programme.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, HLB stated that the issuance comprises Tranche 7 Series 1, amounting to RM350 million, and Tranche 7 Series 2, totalling RM290 million.
"Proceeds from the subordinated notes shall be utilised, without limitation, for HLB's working capital, general banking and other corporate purposes, and the refinancing of any existing borrowings incurred, subordinated debt issued by HLB and/or any existing subordinated notes issued under the programme," it said.
HLB said Tranche 7 Series 1 has a 10-year tenure with a five-year non-call period, while Tranche 7 Series has a 12-year tenure with a seven-year non-call period.
"The coupon rate for Tranche 7 Series 1 is 3.90 per cent per annum and Tranche 7 Series 2 is 4.00 per cent per annum," said HLB, adding that the coupon payment period is six months.
HLB said the Tranche 7 Series 1 call date is June 26, 2031, and Tranche 7 Series 2 is June 27, 2033, with every coupon payment date thereafter.
RAM Rating Services Bhd has assigned an AA1 rating for the subordinated notes programme.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi: Opposition support needed for legal reform bill
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government is counting on opposition support to pass a landmark bill separating the roles of attorney-general and public prosecutor, said government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
The cabinet today discussed the Constitution (Amendment) (No. 2) Bill 2026, which Fahmi described as a major reform the government is committed to delivering.
He said the bill takes into account engagement sessions and findings from a special select committee, and is expected to be debated and passed within the current Parliament sitting.
"The question is whether the opposition will support it to secure the more than two-thirds majority required, given that this is a reform and an improvement effort that is very good for our country's democracy and will address issues raised by Dewan Rakyat members," he told reporters at the communications ministry.
When asked whether there were concerns among party leaders that some government member of parliaments (MPs) might again be absent from the vote, as in a previous constitutional amendment, Fahmi said federal and state developments were different matters.
"At the federal level, we are aware the Unity Government remains intact, and support from component parties continues to allow the Madani government to function effectively," he said.
He said when the bill was previously debated, views were raised by both government backbenchers and the opposition, and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, who led the bill, had taken these into account through a select committee that introduced amendments to address the issues raised.
"This proves that the Madani government listens and brings changes requested by Dewan Rakyat members regardless of party. What is being worked on here could change the landscape.
"This is not only about separating powers between the public prosecutor and the attorney-general, but also about freeing the public prosecutor from the executive," he said.
He said this would ensure the public prosecutor, who holds significant power, could not be controlled or directed by any party for specific purposes.
"So I believe this is a reform that all democrats should support, especially after taking into account the various views of those who had asked for improvements," he said.
"I have seen the bill, and when it was tabled, it included additions as requested. MPs, whether from the government or opposition, need to place our country, our democracy and our Parliament above political games.
"This has nothing to do with politics, this is for the future of our country," he said.
He added that those who attended, were absent, supported or did not support the bill would be recorded in the Parliamentary Hansard.
He said MPs who were absent from the previous vote would need to answer not only to their constituents but to all Malaysians for generations on why they did not support efforts to make the public prosecutor's office independent.
"We hope MPs, regardless of political background or party, will give full support to this constitutional amendment," he said.
The Constitution (Amendment) (No. 2) Bill 2026 was first tabled for first reading on Feb 23 before being referred to a bipartisan Parliamentary Special Select Committee on March 3 for further scrutiny.
The committee, chaired by Azalina and comprising MPs from both government and opposition blocs, finalised its report on May 18, with the revised bill re-tabled when Parliament reconvened on June 22.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UN says Iran nuclear pledge needs 'very strong' verification
Article:
TEHERAN: The UN's nuclear chief said Friday that Iran's pledge not to build a nuclear weapon would need "very strong" verification, as the United States and the Islamic republic negotiate a permanent settlement to end their war.
Iran's nuclear programme is a key sticking point in talks to end the Middle East war, which began in late February with massive US-Israeli strikes.
Iran and the US last week signed a preliminary deal to end the conflict, embarking on negotiations expected to address a host of disputes including the nuclear programme.
But there has been contrasting information from Tehran and Washington on whether UN inspectors will have access to Iranian nuclear facilities.
"I think the objective of this agreement is to ensure that there is no development of nuclear weapons in Iran. The government of Iran has declared quite clearly that this is not their intention," International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) head Rafael Grossi said Friday.
"But of course intentions are not enough. We have to have a very strong verification system in place... as soon as is practicable," he added.
Grossi said the UN's nuclear watchdog had so far "barely initiated" talks with Iran following the agreement with the United States.
The deal specifies that the country's stockpile of enriched uranium should be "downblended" under IAEA supervision.
Before the war the IAEA estimated that Iran had 440 kilograms (970 pounds) of uranium enriched to 60 percent, below the threshold needed to make a bomb, but the fate of that stockpile is unknown after the US and Israel bombed Iranian nuclear facilities last year.
In Tehran meanwhile, people told AFP that despite the diplomatic progress, there had so far been no tangible benefits in everyday life.
"Overall, nothing has improved," said Amir, 28, a government employee. "Life has simply become more difficult."
Mehdi, 35, a content creator, said that "until such changes are felt in people's everyday lives, it is only natural that hope will remain accompanied by doubt, and that anticipation will give way to exhaustion and anxiety."
- 'GOING TO HAPPEN' -
Iran's nuclear programme has long been a source of friction with Western powers, who suspect Tehran is building a bomb despite repeated Iranian denials.
US President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that Tehran had "fully and completely agreed" to allow UN inspectors to return to the country, while on Wednesday Grossi said inspections of Iranian nuclear sites were "going to happen."
Iran however said this week it had no intention of admitting the watchdog.
Tehran agreed a landmark nuclear deal with six big powers in 2015 that placed limits on its nuclear programme in exchange for sanctions relief, but Trump walked away from the agreement during his first term as president.
Iran suspended cooperation with the IAEA last year after the US and Israeli attacks in June.
- OBSTACLES AHEAD -
The dispute threatens to derail efforts to reach a permanent settlement between the US and Iran following months of war, with other key disputes being the Strait of Hormuz and Lebanon.
The strait is a narrow stretch of water between Iran and Oman that leads to the Arabian Sea and the Indian Ocean, making it a chokepoint for crucial energy shipments out of the Gulf.
Iran closed the waterway during the war in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes, and its control of the strait has emerged as key leverage in the negotiations, as the world economy reels from energy shortages.
On Wednesday an attack on a ship in the strait led the UN to suspend an effort to evacuate trapped mariners, many of whom have been stranded on the water since the war began.
The British maritime security agency UKMTO said a cargo ship was "hit on the starboard side by an unknown projectile", but reported no casualties.
Iran has also said it plans to introduce fees for crossing the strait, a scheme vehemently opposed by Washington and most Gulf countries.
Secretary of State Marco Rubio, visiting the Gulf on Wednesday, dismissed the idea of charges, saying it would open the door to "total chaos."
Rubio said the United States wants a deal with Iran but "we don't want a deal at any price."
Tehran has also insisted that a Lebanon ceasefire be included in the regional deal, rankling Israel.
Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem said Israel has "no option but to withdraw completely from every inch of our Lebanese land" that it had invaded, after the pro-Iran group joined the conflict in retaliation for the US-Israeli killing of Iran's supreme leader.
Meanwhile, Lebanon's president Joseph Aoun, who has tried to separate Lebanon from the US-Iran talks.
On Friday, he insisted on Lebanon's eagerness for "any international formula that strengthens the capabilities of its armed forces, preserves its territorial integrity, and prevents its land from becoming an arena for escalation or regional tensions."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mercedes tightens cost-cutting measures, postpones special payment
Article:
STUTTGART: German car manufacturer Mercedes-Benz is tightening its cost-cutting measures, with the DAX-listed group postponing a special payment under the collective agreement, originally scheduled for July, until next year, German Press Agency (dpa) reported.
This is according to a letter to employees in Germany, a copy of which was seen by dpa on Friday.
The total amount involved was initially unclear. The special payment in question is the annual "transformation component," which amounts to 18.4 per cent of the regular individual monthly salary.
The company stated that structural costs in Germany – particularly labour costs – are not competitive by international standards.
At the same time, management intends to discuss an extension of working hours without wage compensation with the works council in the coming weeks, the company added. Under the collective agreement, employees currently work 35 hours a week.
In the letter, the executive board wrote that it was necessary to "continue to work flat out to reduce costs" in order to remain competitive on product prices. "Despite all our efforts, the situation in Germany today is dramatic," the letter stated.
Every new product launch and every assignment of tasks to German sites worsens Mercedes's relative cost position.
Mercedes has been struggling with setbacks for some time. In the first quarter of this year, consolidated profit fell by 17.2 per cent. In 2025, profit had slumped by just under half, from €10.4 billion (US$11.9 billion) to €5.3 billion, following a downturn in 2024.
Tariffs, adverse exchange rate effects, and intense competition in China were cited as factors weighing on the results. Sales volumes and turnover also declined.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Civil service to adopt hybrid work from Aug 1
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Cabinet has agreed to implement the Hybrid Working Day (HBH) initiative as a new norm across the civil service, effective Aug 1.
According to a Public Services Department (PSD) circular, the initiative introduces a more flexible working arrangement for civil servants without reducing their total working hours.
Under the policy, eligible civil servants may work from home or other approved locations for two days a week, while spending the remaining three days in the office.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 26, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed slightly higher on Friday after a late rebound in banking stocks pushed the benchmark index into positive territory.
Telecommunications stocks in the FBM KLCI continued to gain support, with steady buying interest throughout the session.
Although banking stocks were mostly lower during the day, a strong late recovery helped lift the overall market at the close.
Meanwhile, the ringgit strengthened against the US dollar to 4.0850.
In the crypto market, Bitcoin fell to RM245,505.
Ethereum fell to RM6,379, while Solana rose to RM285.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Econpile wins RM48.8mil Pavilion serviced apartment job
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Econpile Holdings Bhd has secured a RM48.8 million contract to undertake specialist bored pile and foundation works for a 74-storey serviced apartment development along Jalan Pavilion here.
The contract was awarded by Vestland Bhd's subsidiary Vestland Infra Sdn Bhd, to Econpile's unit Econpile (M) Sdn Bhd.
The scope covers foundation works for a single tower with basement levels, integrated car parks, retail shop lots and related facilities.
Works are scheduled to run for 16 months, with site mobilisation starting on July 7 this year and completion targeted for November 2027.
The latest job brings Econpile's total new contract awards for the financial year ending June 30, 2026 to RM404 million, while its outstanding order book now exceeds RM500 million, providing earnings visibility through financial year 2028.
Econpile executive director and group chief executive officer Raymond Pang said surpassing RM400 million in new contract wins in FY26 reflects the industry's confidence in the group's specialised geotechnical expertise.
"We look forward to applying our extensive experience in high-rise builds to execute the foundation works for this 74-storey tower project in the Kuala Lumpur city centre," he said in a statement.
Pang said the group remains optimistic about its replenishment pipeline.
"While the current operating environment has its challenges, our strong balance sheet and decades of experience provide resilience to navigate headwinds and ensure smooth project delivery.
"The industry's long-term prospects are stronglyunderpinned by robust high-rise property demand, major infrastructure projects, and continued industrial expansion," he added.
Including the new contract, Econpile's order book currently stands at 25 ongoing projects, with key sites concentrated in the Klang Valley and Penang.
The group's notable ongoing projects include two Penang LRT packages totaling RM96.8 million and a RM98.2 million industrial project in Klang.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Helicopter services specialist GTA gets Bursa nod for listing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: GTA Holdings Bhd has secured approval from Bursa Malaysia for its proposed listing on the Ace Market, paving the way for the aviation maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) specialist to expand its operations and technical capabilities.
The company, through its wholly-owned subsidiary Global Turbine Asia Sdn Bhd, provides MRO-related services for helicopter and fixed-wing engines, parts and components.
Established in 2010, the group serves aviation operators, MRO customers and defence-linked markets, supported by original equipment manufacturer (OEM)-linked relationships within the Safran group and EPI consortium ecosystems.
Based at the Helicopter Centre at Sultan Abdul Aziz Shah Airport in Subang, GTA is strategically positioned within one of Malaysia's key aviation maintenance hubs.
According to its exposure prospectus, the proposed initial public offering (IPO) will involve 329 million shares, comprising 205 million new shares and 124 million existing shares offered for sale.
Of the new shares, 64.57 million will be made available to the public, 12.91 million allocated to eligible directors and employees, while 127.52 million shares will be placed out to institutional and selected investors.
The IPO shares represent about 25.5 per cent of the company's enlarged share capital of 1.29 billion shares.
Proceeds from the public issue will be used to establish a new operating facility, expand helicopter MRO activities in the Middle East, develop capabilities in the maintenance of landing gear, wheels and brakes, support working capital requirements and defray listing expenses.
The company said the new facility will enhance its technical capabilities, support additional certifications, increase manpower and facilitate training initiatives.
GTA managing director and chief executive officer Datuk Nonee Ashirin Mohd Radzi described the approval as a significant milestone in its journey.
This reflects the progress made in building a specialised aviation support business with strong technical expertise and established customer relationships.
For the financial year ended Dec 31, 2024, GTA posted revenue of RM236.65 million and net profit of RM36.61 million.
Revenue contributions came from proactive maintenance service packages at RM91.79 million, corrective maintenance works at RM50.19 million and aviation equipment sales amounting to RM94.67 million.
The company said the IPO is expected to strengthen its operational capabilities, support expansion into complementary aviation support segments and enhance its market reach in Malaysia and overseas.
Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd is the principal adviser, sponsor, sole underwriter and sole placement agent for the listing exercise.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thai police foil bid to smuggle RM1.2mil drugs through Malaysia
Article:
KOTA BARU: Thai authorities have arrested 233 suspected members of drug trafficking syndicates, some believed to have links with Malaysian traffickers, during a month-long anti-drug crackdown in Narathiwat province.
The operation, which ended on June 24, also saw the seizure of narcotics worth more than RM1.2 million that were believed to be destined for Malaysia before being trafficked to other countries.
Narathiwat provincial police commander Major General Prayong Kotsakha said no Malaysians were among those arrested during the operation, which began on May 25.
Dubbed "Narathiwat Fights Drugs", the operation targeted 92 locations across the southern Thai province, including areas bordering Malaysia.
"The syndicates are believed to have planned to smuggle the drugs into Malaysia before trafficking them onwards to other countries.
"However, no Malaysian nationals were arrested during the operation," he told the New Straits Times.
Thai authorities arrested the suspects in 227 drug-related cases.
The drugs seized included 676,288 yaba pills, 500kg of crystal methamphetamine (ice), 180kg of cannabis and 27g of heroin, with the haul estimated to be worth more than RM1.2 million.
Police also seized 20 firearms, which Prayong said were not linked to Malaysia.
"The firearms were kept by the syndicates for self-defence against rival drug trafficking groups or to intimidate those who failed to pay for drugs," he said.
The operation also targeted drug abuse, with action taken against 1,913 drug users.
Of these, 1,768 were sent for rehabilitation while 145 who refused treatment faced legal action.
Prayong said investigations stemming from drug users and their families also led to action against 15 suspected small-scale and major drug dealers.
He said the crackdown reflected Thailand's continued efforts to dismantle drug trafficking networks operating along the Malaysia border and disrupt smuggling routes into neighbouring countries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Civil service pension payments set to hit RM46bil by 2030
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is fine-tuning a new retirement scheme for civil servants, as pension payments are expected to reach RM46.36 billion by 2030.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the proposed new mechanism for the civil service retirement scheme, known as the "contributory permanent appointment" scheme, is still being studied.
He said the system aims to ensure the nation's fiscal position remains strong while continuing to attract top talent, and at the same time safeguarding the welfare of civil servants.
"This retirement scheme creates a fiscal commitment to the government that increases from year to year. A total of RM37.4 billion was spent on pension payments in 2025.
"For 2026, a total of RM42.31 billion is allocated for pension payments, and it is expected to reach RM46.36 billion by 2030," he said.
He said this in a written reply to a question from Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein (BN-Sembrong), published on the Parliament website.
Hishammuddin had asked the government to state the fiscal implications of the new civil service remuneration scheme and whether the restructuring of the pension scheme for new appointments would affect the civil service's ability to attract top talent.
Anwar said the implementation of the Public Service Remuneration System scheme involves an additional financial implication of RM18.91 billion for salary structure improvements.
"However, taking into account the country's challenging economic situation, the salary adjustment is being carried out in phases and applies only to officers who accept the scheme option," he said.
Anwar added that restructuring the new intake pension scheme would not deter top talent from joining the civil service.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit rises further versus US dollar, major and regional currencies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit has continued to strengthen against the US dollar, other major currencies and ASEAN peers on Friday, shrugging off the impact of stronger-than-expected United States (US) economic data.
At 6pm, the local note appreciated to 4.0860/0910 against the greenback from 4.1160/1200 at Thursday's close.
SPI Asset Management managing partner Stephen Innes said the latest US economic data revealed a more uneven picture beneath the headline figures, with growth driven primarily by capital expenditure and government spending, while consumer spending was sharply lower.
"This has encouraged investors to trim aggressive US Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate hike expectations, pulling US yields lower and taking pressure off Asian currencies, including the ringgit," he told Bernama.
According to reports, the US recorded a better-than-expected gross domestic product (GDP) growth of 2.1 per cent in the first quarter of 2026 (1Q 2026), exceeding the consensus forecast of 1.6 per cent.
Meanwhile, the US personal consumption expenditures (PCE) inflation rate came in at 4.1 per cent in May, in line with market expectations.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the final estimate of US 1Q 2026 GDP revealed underlying weakness in the US economy, with consumer spending -- the primary driver of growth -- rising by only 0.5 per cent.
"This suggests that the main reason for higher inflation was mainly the higher cost of fuel. Therefore, raising the interest rate will not bring down the inflation," he said.
At the close, the ringgit was stronger against a basket of major currencies.
It appreciated against the British pound to 5.4013/4079 from 5.4261/4314 at Thursday's close, rose versus the euro to 4.6605/6662 from 4.6762/6807 yesterday, and was firmer vis-à-vis the Japanese yen at 2.5283/5316 from 2.5437/5464 previously.
The local note was also firmer against regional currencies.
It strengthened against the Singapore dollar to 3.1581/1625 from 3.1742/1775 at Thursday's close, and increased versus the Thai baht to 12.2445/2643 from 12.3370/3538 previously.
The ringgit also advanced versus the Indonesian rupiah, rising to 227.9/228.3 from 229.4/229.7 yesterday and strengthened against the Philippine peso to 6.66/6.67 from 6.71/6.72 previously.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Campaign with respect, don't be arrogant, Anwar tells parties
Article:
DENGKIL: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded all parties contesting the Johor election to campaign respectfully, uphold good values and not act arrogantly.
Anwar said three-cornered or multi-cornered contests are a normal feature of a democratic system, but stressed that all parties must ensure their campaigns are carried out respectfully and with regard for the people.
"Three-, four- or even six-cornered contests are normal in a democracy. What matters is that the campaign is conducted politely, with good manners, and that no one becomes conceited.
"For us, we will continue trying to get closer to the people through Pakatan Harapan because of the performance we have shown. Don't be arrogant, don't say, 'Reject this person, reject that person'... no, it is better to work together," he said.
He was speaking to reporters after performing Friday prayers at Masjid Jameatus Solehah Pekan Dengkil here today.
Anwar said all parties should adopt a moderate approach throughout the campaign period, prioritising cooperation and efforts to engage with the public.
"Let us be a little more moderate. Try to serve and reach out to as many people in our society as possible," he said.
The Johor legislative assembly was dissolved on June 1 after receiving the consent of the Tunku Mahkota of Johor, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim
In the 2022 Johor election, Barisan Nasional formed the state government after winning 40 of the 56 state seats, while Pakatan Harapan secured 12 seats, Perikatan Nasional won three, and Muda captured one.
Nomination day is scheduled for tomorrow, followed by early voting on July 7, while polling day will be held on July 11.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia ends slightly higher as late rebound in banking stocks lifts market
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia reversed earlier losses to settle slightly higher on Friday, supported by a late rebound in banking stocks that lifted the benchmark index into positive territory just minutes before the market closed, an analyst said.
At 5pm, the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) index was up by 0.24 per cent, or 3.92 points, to 1,667.74 from Thursday's close of 1,663.82.
The index opened 0.68 of-a-point higher at 1,664.50 and moved between 1,659.74 and 1,668.75 throughout the trading session.
Market breadth, however, was negative with decliners thumping gainers 728 to 334, while 528 counters were unchanged, 1,153 untraded and 65 suspended.
Turnover dwindled to 2.93 billion units valued at RM2.26 billion against 3.18 billion units worth RM2.89 billion on Thursday.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said among the FBM KLCI constituents, the telecommunications sector continued its positive momentum, supported by sustained buying interest throughout the session.
He said although banking stocks traded lower for most of the day, a strong rebound in the final minutes of trading helped reverse earlier losses and provided the necessary support for the index to finish marginally higher.
"Overall, he said market sentiment remained cautious as investors assessed developments in global equity markets.
"Global markets capped a volatile week, with sentiment swinging sharply amid uncertainty surrounding the artificial intelligence (AI) investment theme," he told Bernama.
Mohd Sedek added that investors continued to evaluate corporate earnings, AI-related capital expenditure plans, and broader sector rotation, resulting in heightened volatility across technology stocks.
Meanwhile, Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said key regional indices finished lower following the subdued lead from Wall Street overnight.
Technology stocks remained under pressure across Asia, with Japan and South Korea leading the decline as investors turned more cautious on AI-related semiconductor counters.
Sentiment was further dampened after Apple raised prices across several of its products, citing spiralling memory and storage costs sparked by the rise of AI, prompting investors to reassess valuations across the technology sector.
On the local front, he said although external uncertainties and persistent foreign selling may continue to weigh on sentiment in the near term, the recent correction has improved valuations of many fundamentally sound blue-chip stocks.
Among heavyweights, Maybank rose 8.0 sen to RM10.80, Public Bank and CIMB Group added 2.0 sen each to RM4.83 and RM7.41 respectively, Tenaga Nasional perked up 10 sen to RM14.34, and IHH Healthcare was 3.0 sen easier at RM8.54.
As for the active counters, Zetrix AI was flat at 77 sen, Widad shed half a sen to 1.0 sen, AirAsia X slipped 5.0 sen to RM1.18, Dagang NeXchange lost 2.5 sen to 42 sen, and Tanco was flat to 13.5 sen.
On the gainers' list, United Plantations ticked up 30 sen to RM32.60, Nestle rose 22 sen to RM95.84, Hong Leong Financial Group and Petronas Dagangan climbed 20 sen each to RM18.50 and RM18.60 respectively, and Solarvest jumped 14 sen to RM2.99.
For the top losers, MPI fell RM2.00 to RM47.00, UMS Integration dipped 70 sen to RM7.80, Vitrox slipped 59 sen to RM7.26, F&N slid 48 sen to RM27.70, and UWC shed 30 sen to RM6.30.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Healthcare committee to review PAC findings on rising costs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Joint Ministerial Committee on Private Healthcare Costs said it welcomes a parliamentary report on rising insurance premiums and private hospital charges and will give it "careful consideration" as reforms continue.
The committee, co-chaired by Finance Minister II Senator Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan and Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad, said in a statement that the findings underline the urgency of ongoing healthcare reforms.
The Public Accounts Committee (PAC) report is broadly aligned with the direction of the RESET strategy and reinforces its call to accelerate reform, it said.
The committee said it will give them "careful consideration as it continues to expedite reforms already underway."
The RESET strategy is a collaborative "whole-of-nation" initiative led by the government and Bank Negara Malaysia to tackle rising medical inflation and stabilise private healthcare costs.
The PAC found that some private hospitals separately charged patients for basic items that should ordinarily be included in room charges.
It also found that patients using guarantee letters were, in some cases, charged higher rates than those paying cash or through pay-and-claim arrangements.
PAC member Dr Halimah Ali said yesterday that the committee found evidence of "unbundling", where
hospitals imposed separate charges
for items such as clinical waste disposal, pillowcases and alcohol swabs that should have been included under room or standard service charges.
Since the launch of the RESET strategy, the committee said it has maintained coordinated efforts across the Finance Ministry, Health Ministry, Bank Negara Malaysia and key stakeholders in the healthcare and insurance ecosystem.
The committee said this was to address both immediate and structural drivers of healthcare cost inflation while ensuring continued access to quality healthcare for Malaysians.
"Significant progress has been made across RESET initiatives, with the base medical and health insurance/takaful plan set to enter its pilot implementation phase by the end of July," it said.
Other initiatives include the phased adoption of Diagnosis-Related Group as a provider payment mechanism, greater transparency on prices and drivers of medical inflation, stronger data standards and strengthened dispute resolution channels.
This reflects a whole-of-nation collaboration among policymakers, regulators, payors and providers.
"All parties remain committed to the timely and sustained implementation of the RESET strategy to deliver meaningful outcomes for consumers and more affordable, sustainable private healthcare for Malaysians," the committee said.
The committee continues to closely monitor implementation and will provide further updates at its next meeting in July, it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vestland unit bags RM135mil 'superstructure' contract in Klang
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Vestland Bhd's subsidiary Vestland Resources Sdn Bhd has clinched a RM135 million construction contract from Golden Armani Sdn Bhd.
In a bourse filing, the company said it will undertake superstructure works for one block of a nine-storey stratified factory and related facilities located at Bukit Raja, Klang, Selangor.
The contract covers the design, approval, construction, completion, testing and commissioning of the superstructure works for the industrial development project.
Construction works are scheduled to be completed within 36 months from the commencement date, which will be announced later by Golden Armani.
Vestland said the contract will not have any impact on its issued share capital or shareholding structure as no new shares will be issued.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysians in Tokyo advised to remain vigilant following earthquake in chiba prefecture
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Embassy of Malaysia in Tokyo has advised Malaysians in Japan to remain vigilant and closely monitor local conditions following the magnitude 5.8 earthquake that struck off the northeastern coast of Chiba Prefecture this afternoon.
The embassy, in an earthquake advisory posted on its Facebook page, also advised Malaysians in Japan to refer to the website www.jma.go.jp/jma/indexe.html for the latest updates, warnings and advisories issued by the Japan Meteorological Agency (JMA).
"In the event of an emergency, please contact the Embassy of Malaysia in Tokyo office at +81-3-3476-3840 (office hours), the emergency hotline at +81-80-4322-3366 (after office hours), or email consular.tyo@kln.gov.my or mwtokyo@kln.gov.my," the advisory read.
According to the embassy, there is currently no tsunami threat associated with the earthquake. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM135.63mil in drugs seized, bid to flood local market foiled
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police seized drugs worth an estimated RM135.63 million following three raids and the arrest of seven suspects in Cheras on June 20.
The operation, codenamed Op Horitus, crippled a syndicate believed to have been processing and distributing narcotics to the local market since late last year.
Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the seven suspects, aged between 20 and 55, were arrested during the operation. They comprised six local men and a foreign woman.
The suspects have been remanded to facilitate investigations.
He said police also seized 97.1kg of ecstasy pills, 47.12kg of MDMA (methylenedioxymethamphetamine) and 760 litres of liquid MDMA, all estimated to be worth RM135.63 million.
MDMA is a synthetic psychoactive drug that functions as both a stimulant and a mild hallucinogen powder.
"Preliminary investigations found that the syndicate had been active since November last year and was involved in processing and distributing drugs for the local market," Hussein said at a press conference at the Kuala Lumpur police contingent headquarters today.
He said police also confiscated assets worth RM644,000, including six vehicles believed to be linked to the syndicate.
Hussein said the drugs seized were believed to be capable of supplying about one million addicts if they had reached the market.
In a separate case, a man with the title "Datuk", believed to be the mastermind of a drug trafficking syndicate, was arrested with 290 others in a major operation across Perak, Johor and Kuala Lumpur.
In the operation, codenamed, Op Gaurus, conducted from June 17 to 21, police seized various types of drugs worth RM6.26 million and confiscated several luxury vehicles, including a Maserati Ghibli valued at more than RM300,000.
Hussein said the operation initially focused on six districts in Perak, which are Taiping, Ipoh, Batu Gajah, Tapah, Manjung, and Hilir Perak, before hitting Kuala Lumpur and Johor.
"Among the biggest successes of this operation was the arrest of the syndicate's main mastermind in Taman Midah, Cheras, who holds a 'Datuk' title. The suspect is also believed to be the leader of a secret society," he said.
Hussein said 291 syndicate members were detained, including 21 people who are suspected of involvement in secret society activities.
The syndicate is believed to have been active since mid-2024, operating, processing, storing, and distributing drugs for markets in the Klang Valley, Johor, Perak, and Melaka.
In another development, Hussein said police will use saliva test kits at roadblocks to detect motorists suspected of being under the influence of synthetic liquid drugs marketed as "Piu Piu" and "Magic Mushroom".
He said the NCID is seeking government approval to procure saliva test kits so they can be used at roadblocks by the end of this year.
He said the move comes amid rising concerns over road accidents linked to drivers impaired by substances mixed into vape liquids.
HUssein said a worrying trend had emerged where some motorists unknowingly become intoxicated after inhaling synthetic drugs through e-cigarettes while on the roads.
"These synthetic drugs, marketed under the commercial names 'Piu Piu' and 'Magic Mushroom', are easily obtained online, at entertainment outlets or through drug networks," he said.
He added that such substances had also been detected among secondary school students.
As of June 10, the NCID recorded 168 cases involving synthetic liquid drugs, with 267 arrests, up from 108 cases and 138 arrests last year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Johor BN unveils manifesto with 63 pledges, 200,000 jobs target
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional (BN) today unveiled its manifesto for the Johor election, outlining 63 pledges anchored on six pillars aimed at preserving stability and sustaining the state's development momentum.
The manifesto, themed "Maju Johor, Kestabilan Dikekalkan, Kemajuan Diteruskan" (Advancing Johor, Stability Preserved, Progress Continued), was formulated based on the coalition's experience in administering Johor over the past four years.
Its six pillars cover governance, economic development, social safety nets, basic amenities and necessities, security, cleanliness and environmental sustainability, as well as youth development.
Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the coalition's track record had laid the foundation for the manifesto.
"Drawing on that experience and track record of achievements, I am introducing the Barisan Nasional Johor Manifesto, which outlines 63 pledges anchored on six Johor Progress Pillars," he said in a statement.
Among the key promises are the creation of 200,000 new jobs, including 100,000 premium-income positions for Johor youths, through quality investments, high-technology industries and the expansion of strategic economic sectors.
The coalition also pledged to strengthen the Bantuan Kasih Johor programme through more targeted assistance and introduce a Rental Deposit Payment Scheme to help Johoreans aged below 35 meet the upfront costs of renting a home.
To improve home ownership, BN promised to establish a RM100 million fund to help low-income families build Johor Subsidised Houses on their own land, including second-generation Felda lots.
It also pledged to continue existing housing assistance schemes to help families transition from renting to home ownership.
For businesses, the manifesto proposes first-year business licence fee exemptions for hawkers and small traders, as well as an additional RM100 million in interest-free microfinancing through financial institutions.
It also promises to strengthen opportunities for micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) through business matching, vendor development, capability training and digitalisation.
In education, BN pledged to allocate RM100 million to strengthen the Johor Youth Education Fund, aimed at expanding access to higher education, technical and vocational education and training (TVET), professional certification and future-ready skills training.
The coalition also promised to continue the RM200 Johor Youth Student Incentive for students pursuing studies at public higher education institutions, polytechnics and community colleges.
Among its social initiatives, BN pledged to introduce the Semarak Isya' programme in every state constituency as a continuation of the Semarak Subuh Johor programme to revitalise mosques and suraus.
Onn Hafiz said Johor's development must balance economic growth, public welfare and the strengthening of societal values.
"This pledge is not merely a promise, but is built on administrative experience, a proven track record and efforts to bring greater progress to Johor."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Resilient dollar takes a step back, markets still wary of yen intervention risk
Article:
LONDON: The dollar was a touch softer against other major currencies on Friday as fresh economic data and Federal Reserve comments led markets to pare rate-hike bets, allowing the yen trading in an intervention danger zone to find firmer ground.
The greenback was still poised to end the week higher and remains on track for its best month since July 2025, with gains of almost 2.5 per cent.
Thursday's data showing a key measure of US inflation met economists' expectations which tempered rate-hike bets. It is expected to stall rather than derail the dollar's near-term march higher.
"We have had a bit of profit taking, maybe because of month-end but I think this move in the dollar could extend a bit more," said Nick Kennedy, a currency strategist at Lloyds in London.
"In aggregate, rate differentials are driving things again."
The dollar index, which measures the greenback's strength against a basket of six currencies, was down 0.2 per cent at 101.31. It remains within sight of more than one-year highs hit earlier in the week.
The euro was 0.15 per cent higher at US$1.1385, while the British pound was up 0.1 per cent at US$1.3201.
Interest rate expectations for major economies have returned to the driving seat in global currency markets, with a strong US economy and hawkish signalling at the June Federal Reserve press conference boosting the dollar.
US money markets are fully pricing in a one quarter-point rate hike by year-end.
Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said on Thursday that while inflation pressures are likely to moderate this year, they remain too high.
STILL IN THE DANGER ZONE
Japan's yen strengthened 0.1 per cent against the dollar to 161.62 yen per dollar, rising from a two-year trough of 161.95 on Thursday. Breaching the 161.96 mark would take it to its weakest level since 1986.
The weaker side of 160 is considered by many in the market as a line in the sand for Japanese officials.
Some banks accelerated their timeline for rate hikes from the Bank of Japan after data showed on Friday that core inflation in Tokyo accelerated in June, providing additional support for the yen.
The data suggests "second-round effects from higher oil prices are increasing, while Bank of Japan officials are sounding more hawkish," analysts from ING wrote in a research note.
"With core prices likely to accelerate going forward, we have brought forward our BOJ rate-hike call to October from December."
Elsewhere, the Australian dollar eased 0.3 per cent to US$0.6895. Bitcoin was up almost 2 per cent at US$60,454, recovering some losses after reaching its lowest since September 2024 earlier this week.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Datuk' gang leader among 291 arrested in RM6.26mil drug bust [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A man with the title "Datuk", believed to be the mastermind of a drug trafficking syndicate, was arrested with 290 others in a major operation across Perak, Johor and Kuala Lumpur.
During Op Gaurus, conducted from June 17 to 21, police seized various types of drugs worth RM6.26 million and confiscated several luxury vehicles, including a Maserati Ghibli valued at more than RM300,000.
Bukit Aman Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) Director, Datuk Hussein Omar Khan, said the operation initially focused on six districts in Perak, which are Taiping, Ipoh, Batu Gajah, Tapah, Manjung, and Hilir Perak, before hitting Kuala Lumpur and Johor.
"Among the biggest successes of this operation was the arrest of the syndicate's main mastermind in Taman Midah, Cheras, who holds a 'Datuk' title.
"The suspect is also believed to be the leader of a secret society," he said at a press conference at the Kuala Lumpur Contingent Police Headquarters (IPK) here today.
Hussein said 291 syndicate members were detained, including 21 people who are suspected of involvement in secret society activities.
He added that the syndicate is believed to have been active since mid-2024, operating, processing, storing, and distributing drugs for markets in the Klang Valley, Johor, Perak, and Melaka.
Hussein said the seized drugs included MDMA, ecstasy pills, and ketamine, with a total value of RM6.26 million.
"The syndicate also possessed various equipment and chemicals used to process drugs.
"We believe the drug supply was targeted at entertainment centres," he said.
In addition to the drug seizures, police also confiscated various assets, including several vehicles, with a total value exceeding RM700,000.
Hussein said that crippling the syndicate, including its network of processing laboratories and drug storage facilities, dealt a major blow to drug trafficking activities in the country.
"The police will continue to intensify operations to track down and bust other active drug syndicates," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Family mourns boy, 5, who fell from Penang condominium
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A family is mourning its 5-year-old son, who allegedly fell from a condominium in Teluk Kumbar, after a final light-hearted moment together.
The family of the boy, believed to have fallen from the 24th floor of a condominium on Thursday night, described him as cheerful, affectionate and full of life.
His father, Efwan Reza Rusdizal, 40, said Emyr Nader Redza, the youngest of three siblings, was a dominant yet sensitive child who often brightened the family's days with his playful personality.
Fondly known as "bos kecil", Emyr loved drawing cartoons and had a remarkable ability to reproduce images he saw, displaying artistic talent despite his young age.
"To us, he was always cheerful. We could already see his talent through his drawings.
"There was nothing unusual about him yesterday. He was his usual self and showed no changes in recent days.
"We never expected this to happen, but we accept Allah's will. Many have told us he is a child of paradise, and we pray he will intercede for us in the hereafter," he said when met at the Penang Hospital Forensic Department today.
Efwan said the family's last memory of Emyr was of him jokingly wearing his elder sister's school headscarf about an hour before the incident.
"I put my daughter's school headscarf on him before taking a photo. He looked just like his cousin, and we all laughed.
"We never imagined that would become our last memory together.
"It was such a simple family moment. But today, when I saw his cousin at the forensic department, I couldn't stop thinking about yesterday," he said.
Efwan said he and his wife, Nurul Nadiah Mohamad, 40, had lived apart for almost two years due to her work commitments in Kuala Lumpur, but maintained close family ties through daily video calls.
"We never missed our daily video calls over the past two years. Sometimes, during weekends, we would call each other five or six times.
"We accept this fate and hope the public will refrain from speculating about the incident and allow our family the space to grieve.
"My son passed away on a blessed day and in a blessed month. I ask everyone to pray for him," he added.
Emyr's body will be taken to Petaling Jaya, Selangor, for burial after a post-mortem examination at Penang Hospital.
Earlier reports said the boy was found dead on the fifth-floor podium of the condominium after he was believed to have fallen from his family's unit on the 24th floor.
Members of the public discovered him at 11.30pm and alerted authorities through the MERS 999 emergency line.
Initial police and forensic investigations found no evidence of foul play.
Southwest district police chief Assistant Commissioner Anuwal Ab Wahab is expected to issue a media statement on the incident.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tax exemption on services charges removes major cost concern: Industry body
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The service tax exemption on service charges and sinking fund contributions for non-residential buildings removes a major cost concern for industry stakeholders, said an industry body.
The Malaysian Institute of Property and Facility Managers (MIPFM) said the exemption reduces operating costs for property owners, joint management bodies and building tenants, while providing greater certainty in planning operational and maintenance expenditure.
MIPFM president Ishak Ismail said decision was the result of constructive engagement between the government and industry stakeholders.
"This decision demonstrates the government's commitment to evidence-based policymaking through constructive engagement with stakeholders," he said in a statement.
He said MIPFM had participated in several rounds of consultation with the Finance Ministry and the Customs Department, where it consistently highlighted the concerns and recommendations of property and facility management professionals.
He said the exemption demonstrates the value of continued dialogue in shaping practical and balanced policies that support the industry while safeguarding the interests of property owners and the public.
MIPFM said it remains committed to working closely with the government and relevant agencies to strengthen industry practices, promote sound policies and raise professional standards in property and facility management.
Under the Strata Management Act 2013, owners of both residential and non-residential stratified properties such as offices, retail lots and mixed-use commercial buildings must pay maintenance charges and sinking fund contributions.
Prior to the exemption, sinking fund contributions for commercial parcels were subject to eight per cent service tax.
If a building collects RM1 million annually in maintenance charges and sinking fund contributions, property owners would save RM80,000 under the service tax exemption.
Maintenance charges are used for the day-to-day upkeep and operation of the building, while sinking fund contributions are set aside for major repairs and long-term capital replacement.
They are typically fixed at a minimum of 10 per cent of the maintenance charges and are collected by the JMB or MC.
The sinking fund is used for major works, including lift replacement, roof repairs, fire safety system upgrades and facode or infrastructure improvements.
For non-residential properties, the sinking fund is particularly important because commercial buildings generally experience higher wear and tear due to heavier usage and frequent tenant turnover.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Psychiatrist encounters increasing number of depressed and high risks children
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: A consultant psychiatrist told the court that there was an increasing number of children and adolescents suffering from depression and are at risk of self-harm or suicide.
On the 78th day of the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir, Dr Wong Haw Huo described encountering such trends in his daily work.
The 75th witness was replying to lawyer Shahlan Jufri's question on his additional training in Child and Adolescent Psychiatry.
"I developed a special interest in understanding children and adolescents ever since my postgraduate period, and I took the initiative to apply to the university for extended clinical supervision and training in the child psychiatry unit.
"It is my duty as a doctor to help these vulnerable groups return to school and achieve a better quality of life," he testified before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan.
Shahlan is representing Zara Qairina's mother Noraidah Lamat.
The 39-year-old expert witness, who has 12 years of experience in psychiatry, manages between 120 and 280 cases involving children every year.
He said between 10 and 15 per cent involve those with autism; about 30 per cent are those with attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder; 30 to 40 per cent are those with depression and anxiety disorder; at least 30 per cent suffer from self-harm behaviours, including self-cutting; 15 to 20 per cent involve those with suicidal thoughts or behaviour; five to eight per cent are those with aggressive or violent behaviour; and more than 80 per cent involve those who refuse to attend school, have emotional difficulties or behavioural difficulties.
Dr Wong said the cases he most encountered involved adolescent girls with mood and behavioural difficulties, non-suicidal self-injury and suicidal risks.
"Every individual sent to the clinic is unique, and the cases usually involve more than one condition," he said.
This is where, he said, intervention by the parents and school, is important.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Babysitter remanded three days over death of 11-month-old girl in Bedong [WATCH]
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: A babysitter has been remanded for three days to assist investigations into the death of an 11-month-old girl.
Magistrate Muhammad Syaiful Akmal Mohamad Razi approved the remand application against the woman, who is in her 30s, from today until Sunday.
Media reports yesterday said the babysitter was
detained
by police after being called in to assist investigations into the death of the infant, who had been under her care at her home in Taman Permaipura, Bedong.
The baby girl died at 10.42am yesterday while receiving treatment at Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital (HSB) in Alor Setar.
Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said the victim's father received a call from the babysitter at 2.15pm on Monday, informing him that his daughter had fallen off a chair while being fed and had lost consciousness.
He said the infant's parents were staff members at a private hospital here, and that the victim was the couple's only child.
The case is being investigated under Section 31 of the Child Act.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi: Modest but festive National Day celebration planned this year
Article:
PUTRAJAYA:The government will stage a more modest National Day celebration this year without compromising the festive spirit, while unveiling 'Kesejahteraan Dinikmati Malaysia Madani' as the official theme for the 2026 celebrations.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the main event on Aug 31, 2026, will be held at Dataran Putrajaya as the government seeks to organise a celebration that remains festive while adopting a more modest approach.
"I am pleased to announce that the 2026 National Day celebration will be held at Dataran Putrajaya on a more modest scale, but it will not be any less festive," he said.
Fahmi added that the official Malaysia Madani logo will be retained as the primary identity for the celebrations, reflecting the aspiration to build an inclusive, harmonious and prosperous nation.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Samenta calls for shift from welfare to innovation-led SME support
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia should shift its approach to supporting small and medium enterprises (SMEs), with greater emphasis on innovation, productivity and competitiveness, the Small and Medium Enterprises Association Malaysia (Samenta) said.
Its national president, Datuk William Ng, said it was time to stop treating SMEs as welfare recipients and instead build an ecosystem that rewards value creation and long-term growth.
He said SMEs remain critical to Malaysia's economy, accounting for more than 97 per cent of all businesses and about half of national employment, acting as a key socio-economic buffer across rural and urban areas.
However, he warned that strong gross domestic product (GDP) growth does not necessarily reflect healthier businesses.
"GDP measures output. It doesn't measure margins. Even if an SME is selling more (contributing to GDP), the cost of raw materials, electricity and rent has gone up.
"If your revenue goes up by five per cent but your costs go up by 10 per cent, every single sen that you make that contributes to the GDP is actually a step towards bankruptcy," he said in a statement in conjunction with World MSME Day.
Ng said many SMEs are facing structural pressures beyond cost inflation, including shifts in technology, supply chains, consumer behaviour and workforce expectations, which many are not prepared for.
He said the government support has too often focused on grants and cheap financing rather than addressing deeper business challenges.
"The real deficit is an execution and business model deficit. Cheap loans do not solve obsolete business models," he said, adding that subsidies can delay necessary restructuring rather than drive transformation.
Ng called for a shift from "poverty alleviation to wealth creation", urging policymakers, financial institutions and industry players to focus on capability-building, innovation and productivity instead of short-term survival support.
Among the reforms proposed, Samenta highlighted the need for banking systems to recognise intangible assets such as intellectual property and technology as valid collateral, alongside land and physical assets.
It also called for a review of regulatory burdens, a reassessment of the role of government-linked companies in commercial markets and a more balanced view of entrepreneurship as a pathway to building scalable businesses rather than simply a solution to unemployment.
Ng added that Malaysia's labour framework needs updating, arguing that existing rules remain tied to an industrial-era model based on fixed working hours, which no longer reflects an economy driven by automation and skills.
He said Malaysia must move beyond its traditional role as a low-cost manufacturing hub.
He said the shift should be from "Made in Malaysia" to "Made by Malaysia", emphasising the need for SMEs to own intellectual property, build their own brands and develop original products for global markets.
"Stop treating small businesses like welfare cases," he added, calling for policies that empower entrepreneurs to compete globally and drive higher-value growth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Husband recounts missed goodbye before wife's fatal crash
Article:
GERIK: "Every morning before leaving for work, we would shake hands and kiss each other goodbye. But yesterday, we were both in a rush and didn't get the chance."
Those heartbreaking words were all Hazrul Jalaluddin could utter as he recalled the final morning he shared with his wife, Zuraida Abu Bakar, before she was killed in a crash that also claimed the life of a police inspector in Lenggong yesterday.
Hazrul, 45, said his wife hurried out of their home to attend a course at Kolej Kemahiran Tinggi Mara (KKTM) Lenggong, while he was rushing to a meeting in Ipoh.
Neither of them knew it would be the last time they would see each other.
Zuraida, 47, a teacher at Gerik Vocational College, was among six teachers travelling in a Honda Odyssey when it collided with a Toyota Vios at Km29 of the Kuala Kangsar-Gerik-Pengkalan Hulu Road, near a restaurant here.
She died at the scene together with Inspector Melvindar Singh Gamum Singh, 41, of the Pengkalan Hulu district police headquarters. Five other teachers travelling with Zuraida were injured.
Speaking outside the Forensic Unit of Gerik Hospital today, Hazrul said he first learnt of the tragedy through a telephone call from a teacher while attending a meeting in Ipoh.
He recalled that his wife had behaved differently in the days leading up to the tragedy.
"Our routine was simple. Whether she left first or I did, we would always shake hands and kiss before going to work. But yesterday, she rushed out because she wanted to carpool with a friend.
"For the past week, I had this strange feeling that I was going to lose her. Even her expression looked different, especially when she was asleep.
"I love going on motorcycle rides and she would usually refuse, saying she was too tired. But lately, whenever I asked, she agreed without hesitation, even when it looked like it was going to rain," he said.
The father of three said the loss had devastated the family, particularly their youngest child, a 12-year-old daughter who was especially close to her mother.
"We have been married for 20 years. Just last month, we celebrated our 20th wedding anniversary.
"Our youngest is very attached to her mother. It will be the hardest for her," said Hazrul, an assistant agricultural officer with the Rubber Industry Smallholders Development Authority (Risda).
Family members, relatives and teachers from Gerik Vocational College gathered at the hospital's forensic unit to pay their last respects.
Zuraida's remains will be taken to Masjid Nurussalam in Batu 4, Jalan Kuala Rui, for funeral prayers after Friday prayers before being laid to rest at the Kampung Telok Batu Islamic Cemetery in Lenggong.
Meanwhile, Inspector Melvindar's remains will be managed by the Pengkalan Hulu district police headquarters before being sent to his family in Klang, Selangor.
Two of the five injured teachers remain in serious condition and are receiving treatment at Raja Permaisuri Bainun Hospital in Ipoh and Taiping Hospital.
Police said the six teachers were returning from a
course
at KKTM Lenggong when the collision occurred.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Govt to build more housing for civil servants
Article:
DENGKIL: The government will build more housing for civil servants on government-owned land at lower rental rates to help ease their cost of living burden, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Anwar said the move followed rising rental rates that have affected civil servants, particularly in major cities.
"During my recent visits to Penang, Perak, Negri Sembilan, Johor and Kuala Lumpur, I found that civil servants are facing a serious problem — rising rental rates.
"Even though we have raised their salaries by between 15 per cent and a maximum of 30 per cent, sometimes in places like Johor Baru, Kuala Lumpur, Seremban and Ipoh, it is still not enough to cover the rising cost of rent," he said after performing Friday prayers at Masjid Jameatus Solehah in Dengkil today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Labuan Airport shuts runway after bombs found near tarmac, flights cancelled and diverted until clearance
Article:
LABUAN, June 26 — Flight operations at Labuan Airport have been disrupted following the temporary closure of its runway after the discovery of bombs 150-metre from the runway.
A Notice to Airmen (NOTAM), as posted at the Malaysia Airport Facebook, has been issued by the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) at 5pm today for the temporary closure of the runway until further notice.
The RMAF and airport authorities are currently conducting a joint inspection of the runway, which will only be reopened after the inspection is completed and the necessary safety clearance has been issued.
The closure has resulted in the cancellation of flights from Labuan to Miri and Kota Kinabalu, while several other arriving and departing flights have been diverted, delayed or are subject to operational changes.
Passengers travelling to and from Labuan are advised to check with their respective airlines for the latest flight status and travel arrangements before proceeding to the airport.
Further updates will be provided as more information becomes available.
Bernama’s checks at the Labuan Airport found that flights from 4.05pm, 5.15pm, 6.05pm, 6.15pm and 7.25pm destined to Kuala Lumpur, Kota Kinabalu and Miri have been affected. — Bernama

Title: Customs bust RM52.9m AI server syndicate at KLIA Free Trade Zone, probe under Strategic Trade Act
Article:
SEPANG, June 26 — The Customs Department (Customs) has seized 72 servers containing advanced artificial intelligence (AI) chips, valued at an estimated RM52.9 million, at the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Free Trade Zone on suspicion of violating the Strategic Trade Act 2010.
KLIA Customs director Zulkifli Muhammad said the seizure was made during an inspection by a team from the KLIA Customs Department’s Enforcement Division at a warehouse in the KLIA Free Trade Zone at about 4.30pm on June 5.
“Preliminary investigations found that the servers were to be re-exported to a country in Asia.
“The servers were declared as ‘computer components’ to avoid detection by the authorities,” he told a press conference here today.
He said the syndicate is also believed to have used Malaysia as a transit point to ensure the servers could be exported to their final destination without facing any restrictions.
Zulkifli said all 72 servers had been seized for further investigation as they were suspected of being subject to transactions involving unlisted items and restricted activities under the Strategic Trade Act 2010.
Under directives issued by the Strategic Trade Secretariat (STS) of the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti), any party intending to export, tranship or transit advanced AI chips must notify the STS at least 30 days in advance if they know or have reasonable grounds to suspect the chips may be used for restricted activities.
He said the freight forwarding company involved had been summoned to assist in the investigation, with the case being investigated under Section 12 of the Strategic Trade Act 2010.
In a separate case, Zulkifli said Customs foiled an attempt to smuggle 4,760 vape liquid cartridges suspected of containing methamphetamine, worth RM1.19 million, on June 10. The shipment was believed to have been destined for a neighbouring country.
“The syndicate declared the shipment as computer spare parts to mislead the authorities. Further inspection found the vape cartridges had been concealed inside computer casings before being shipped via a cargo service,” he said.
He said investigations are ongoing to identify those involved, as the sender is believed to have used a false address.
The case is being investigated under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, and so far, three employees of the freight forwarding company have been called in to assist with the investigation. — Bernama

Title: Tengku Putri Arafiah, sister of Sultan Sharafuddin, dies at 72, laid to rest at Shah Alam mausoleum
Article:
SHAH ALAM, June 26 — Tengku Putri Arafiah Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah, the younger sister of the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, who died at her residence this morning, was laid to rest at the Shah Alam Royal Mausoleum here at about 3.30pm today.
Earlier, funeral prayers were held at the Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Mosque after Friday prayers, led by Grand Imam Datuk Muhammad Farhan Wijaya.
Sultan Sharafuddin and the Raja Muda of Selangor, Tengku Amir Shah, attended the funeral prayers before witnessing the burial.
The Selangor ruler was accompanied by the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, at the burial.
Their Royal Highnesses also took part in the sprinkling of rose water after the talkin (reminder) was recited by Selangor Deputy Mufti Datuk Dr Miszairi Sitiris.
Among those in attendance were the Sultan’s younger brothers, Tengku Laksamana of Selangor Tengku Sulaiman Shah and Tengku Panglima Raja of Selangor Tengku Ahmad Shah, as well as the Tengku Ampuan of Pahang, Tunku Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah.
A source from the Selangor Palace confirmed that Tengku Putri Arafiah died at her residence in Section 12 here due to age-related illness. She was 72. — Bernama

Title: Petronas manager’s alleged leak of confidential report to Petros could jeopardise talks, court told
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — The Sessions Court was told today that the actions of a former manager at Petronas in leaking confidential information of the oil company to Petroleum Sarawak Berhad (Petros) could jeopardize the negotiation process between the two oil companies.
Petronas General Manager Syakirah Mustapa, 51, said that if reports regarding Petronas’s operational and financial performance were leaked to a competitor, they would be able to know the current operational and financial performance of Petronas Upstream for the first quarter, as well as the overall estimates for the year 2024.
“The information includes financial strength, operational performance, production volume and issues, health, safety, and environmental issues, oil and gas discoveries, project investments, and company divestment issues such as in Sudan.
“The leak of this information could jeopardize the negotiation process between Petronas and Petros, potentially leading to negotiations that are disadvantageous to Petronas,” she said when reading her witness statement during the trial of Mohd Khairul Akmal Mohd Jasni, who is charged with attempting to disclose the report to Petros.
The second prosecution witness added that as the individual entrusted with maintaining the confidentiality of company documents, Mohd Khairul Akmal had indeed betrayed the trust given to him if it is proven that he leaked or attempted to leak the report to a third party.
“No permission was ever given to him (Mohd Khairul Akmal) to use the report for personal purposes or to share it with a third party.
“The third party is Datuk Janin Girie, who is a former Petronas employee and currently holds the position of Chief Executive Officer of Petros, while Azha Jalil is the Chief Financial Officer of Petros. I am not sure if Mohd Khairul Akmal knows both individuals,” Syakirah said during the examination-in-chief by Deputy Public Prosecutor Noor Syafina Mohd Radzuan.
Syakirah said that Mohd Khairul Akmal’s role, who held the position of Business Unit Performance Manager at Petronas for 22 months, was to manage the preparation of financial performance reports for the upstream business on a monthly, quarterly, and annual basis.
She said the process of preparing the report involved two main groups: Business Performance Improvement (BPI) and Business Planning and Performance Reporting (BPPR).
“The report is controlled and initiated by BPI and given to BPPR to complete the relevant sections. The group consists of myself, Mohd Khairul Akmal, and seven other individuals,” said Syakirah, who heads BPPR.
The witness said Mohd Khairul Akmal was not allowed to share the report with any external party other than those involved in its preparation, but the accused is suspected of leaking company information through Petronas’s email.
In addition, Syakirah said she had conducted a comparison and confirmed that the document sent by Mohd Khairul Akmal to a third party through his email was identical to the original report titled “Q1 2024 Upstream Business Performance – Operational and Financial”.
“The document was presented by myself and Jasmin Nisha to the Upstream Leadership Team on May 10, 2024, without any changes to its original content,” the witness explained.
Meanwhile, during cross-examination by lawyer Louis Liaw Vern Xien representing Mohd Khairul Akmal, the witness said she was shocked by Mohd Khairul Akmal’s actions because he had performed his duties well throughout his tenure in the position.
Mohd Khairul Akmal, 41, is charged with attempting to disclose information, specifically a document titled “Q1 2024 Upstream Business Performance Operational & Financial” to Petros. The document was obtained while executing his duties as the Petronas Business Unit Performance Manager, at a condominium unit in Jalan Pinang, here, between 3:19 pm and 3:21 pm on June 8, 2024.
The charge is framed under Section 203A(1) of the Penal Code, read together with Section 511 of the same Code, which provides for a maximum fine of RM1 million or imprisonment for up to one year, while Section 511 carries a prison sentence of not more than half of the maximum term under Section 203A(1), upon conviction.
The trial before Judge Mazuliana Abdul Rashid will resume on July 7. — Bernama

Title: Johor BN promises 200,000 quality jobs, RM100m housing and education funds in state election manifesto
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) today pledged to create 200,000 quality jobs and channel RM100 million each towards affordable housing and youth education as it unveiled its manifesto for the 16th Johor state election.
The manifesto, themed “
Maju Johor
, Stability Maintained, Progress Continued”, contains 63 pledges built around six Maju Johor pillars, which Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said were based on the coalition’s four years in government and the needs of the people.
Among its flagship promises are a target of creating 200,000 quality jobs for Johoreans, a RM100 million fund to expand access to subsidised home ownership, and another RM100 million allocation to strengthen the Johor Youth Education Fund.
The coalition also pledged to introduce a rental deposit payment scheme for young people, continue first-home purchase, housewarming and rental assistance, waive first-year business licence fees for hawkers and small traders, and increase interest-free microfinancing by RM100 million.
“
Alhamdulillah
, four years ago,
Bangsa Johor
placed its trust in BN to lead this state. During that period, we worked hard and achieved much together.
“Drawing on that experience and track record, I am introducing the Johor BN Manifesto, which outlines 63 commitments based on the six
Maju Johor
pillars,” Onn Hafiz said in a Facebook post today.
He said the coalition also aimed to strengthen the Johor Compassionate Assistance (BKJ) programme through more targeted aid and boost the participation of Johor’s micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) in strategic investment supply chains.
“On the economic front, we also want to boost the participation of Johor’s micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) in strategic investment supply chains, while strengthening the Johor Youth Education Fund with a RM100 million allocation, continuing incentives for Johor students and implementing the
Semarak Isya’
Programme,” he said.
Onn Hafiz said the commitments were not simply campaign promises but were backed by BN’s experience in governing the state.
“These are not merely promises. They are built on our governing experience, a proven track record and our commitment to taking Johor further forward,” he said.
He also called on Johoreans to grant BN another mandate to continue its development agenda.
“The seeds we planted are now beginning to bear fruit. Do not let Johor’s momentum for progress come to a halt halfway. We laid the foundation, we are seeing the results, and now it is time to complete what we started.
“Come,
Bangsa Johor
! Give BN a full mandate to continue what has already been proven for a more advanced, prosperous and harmonious Johor,” he said.
BN won 40 of Johor’s 56 state assembly seats in the 2022 election to form the state government.
Nomination day for this year’s election is tomorrow, with early voting on July 7 and polling set for July 11.

Title: Govt aims to table AG-Public Prosecutor separation Bill this parliamentary sitting, seeks bipartisan backing, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — The government is targeting the current parliamentary sitting to table a Bill for debate and approval on the proposed separation of the roles of the Attorney General and the Public Prosecutor, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today.
He said Putrajaya remains firmly committed to the proposed reform, which is seen as a significant step towards strengthening institutional independence and governance.
“This is a major reform initiative that the government is committed to implementing. Stakeholder engagement sessions and the findings of the Select Committee have been taken into account.
“The government expects to table the Bill for debate and approval within the current parliamentary sitting,” the government spokesman told reporters during his weekly post-Cabinet press conference today.
However, Fahmi said it remains to be seen whether the Opposition will support the Bill in order to secure the required two-thirds majority, given that it is a reform intended to strengthen democracy and address long-standing concerns raised in Parliament.
He urged all Members of Parliament to support the constitutional amendment, stressing that it should be treated as a national reform rather than a partisan issue.
He said lawmakers must prioritise the country’s democratic institutions over political considerations, emphasising that the reform would ensure the Public Prosecutor is free from executive influence.
“If examined closely, this is what will ensure that the Public Prosecutor, who holds significant powers, is not controlled or directed by any party for specific purposes.
